title: Modified ZN Staining Protocol
author: Asar Khan
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.tb2eiqe

steps.instruction: The stool sample was Spread evenly on the middle of the slide with constant rotational movement. a 00:10:00, (5 to 10 minutes) for rotational movement undetermined, (Amount of stool sample)
The slides were than placed on dryer with smeared surface upwards to air-dried them. Undetermined 00:10:00, minutes
The dried smear was fixed with absolute methanol. 00:05:00, or (3-5 minutes)
Now, the Carbol-fuchsine solution was added to the slide to cover the whole smear. a Carbol-Fuchsin Contributed by users 00:20:00, minutes

The slides were washed gently with tap water with the help of a dropper. Do not expose the slides to the high pressure of tap water directly, rather it will be better to use a dropper for washing the slides.
After washing the slide, decolorizer (Acid Alcohol) twas added o the smear and the slide washed again with tap water. Acid Alcohol Contributed by users undetermined, or 4-6 drops

Then the counter stain (Methylene Blue) was added and left for 5 minutes and then washed the slide with clean water. Methylene Blue Gold Biotechnology Catalog #M-680 00:05:00, minutes wait for methylene blue

The smear was examined with the help of a compound microscope with 40x and 100x (immersion oil lens) objective and scanned throughly for parasite identification. a (Olympus, CH20i, Biological microscope , Anti-fungus treated optics , Built to last- Superior build quality, https://s3.amazonaws.com/pr-journal/4jg2t76.jpg )
The back side of the slides were cleaned with a tissue paper and put in the draining rack to air-dry. 00:05:00, minutes, wait for slide to dry
